title: Small Business Programs
summary: This Treasury webpage provides an overview of government small business support programs including the State Small Business Credit Initiative (SSBCI), Small Business Lending Fund (SBLF), Community Development Financial Institutions (CDFI) Fund, and related initiatives. The page is informational in nature and describes program structures and purposes rather than announcing market-moving news or policy changes.
key_points: SSBCI supports state programs that provide loans and investments to creditworthy small businesses unable to access needed capital
SBLF invested over $4.0 billion in 332 institutions to encourage small business lending through community banks
CDFI Fund promotes economic revitalization in low-income communities through mission-driven lenders and community development organizations

CDFI Fund operates multiple programs: Community Development Financial Institutions Program, New Markets Tax Credit Program, CDFI Bond Guarantee Program, Bank Enterprise Award Program, and Native American CDFI Assistance Program
Office of Small and Disadvantaged Business Utilization assists various small business categories with Treasury contracting procedures
Interagency Community Investment Committee provides resource guides for small businesses in historically underserved communities
why_it_matters: This source is a static informational page about existing Treasury small business programs. It does not announce new policy changes, provide market-moving data, or discuss current macroeconomic conditions. It has no relevance to trading or investment decisions related to market structure or macro catalysts.
important_numbers: 332 (number of institutions invested in through SBLF)
